content: The working memory capacity limits that bound an AI agent’s reasoning performance can be framed as an emergent, self-regulating curiosity filter: only information that triggers intrinsic curiosity (measured via prediction error in ICM/RND frameworks) gets prioritized for retention in the agent’s limited active working memory, rather than all incoming observations being stored. This tradeoff is not a bug, but an adaptive feature that prevents reasoning overload in long-horizon interactive tasks.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
trigger_topic: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
